commit.message:
fix: graceful fallback when GitHub rejects inline review comments (#50)

## Problem

PR #49 review failed with 422 because the CLI generated inline comments
with line numbers not in the PR diff. The entire review was lost.

## Fix

When `post_review()` gets a 422 and has inline comments, it retries
without them — appending the comment text to the review body instead.
The review still gets posted, feedback isn't lost.

## Context

This is a natural consequence of the token refactor (#40) — the CLI
writes review JSON and the orchestrator posts it. The CLI doesn't know
which lines are in the diff, so it may guess wrong. This fallback
ensures the review always gets posted.

commit.message:
fix: register SIGTERM handler before initial sync cycle

Move signal handler registration to the top of main() so that
SIGTERM sent by Docker during the initial startup sync (which can
run for hours across 300+ TLDs) is correctly intercepted.

Previously the handler was only registered after the blocking
scheduler was created, meaning containers receiving SIGTERM during
the startup cycle would ignore it and keep running until SIGKILL
(30-minute grace period). This caused 3 simultaneous worker
containers during rolling deploys.

fix(maglev): fix truncation chain conversion by recursing before checking inputs

The mark_truncated function checked is_provably_i32 on inputs BEFORE
recursively processing them, preventing Generic->Int32 conversion for
chains longer than 1 node. For a chain like:
  GenericBitwiseOr -> GenericSubtract -> GenericAdd -> Phi
the backward walk failed at GenericSubtract because GenericAdd hadn't
been converted yet.

Fix: recursively call mark_truncated on inputs FIRST, then check
is_provably_i32 (inputs may now be in replacements map). This allows
the full arithmetic chain to be converted to wrapping Int32 ops.

Also adds early-return guard for nodes already in replacements to
prevent infinite recursion.

as the entire (n + i*3 - 1) | 0 chain converts to Int32 ops.

commit.message:
Tab completion: parse tab-separated descriptions as animated display-only suffix (#470)

Bash completion scripts can embed descriptions in completion strings
using tab separators (e.g. `option\tdescription`). This adds support for
parsing and displaying those descriptions without inserting them.

## Behavior
- Text before the first `\t` is the completion value (inserted on
accept)
- Text after the first `\t` is a display-only description shown as a
visual suffix in the grid
- Multiple `\t`-separated segments after the first are treated as
animation frames, cycled at 24 fps
- Fuzzy matching and common-prefix detection operate only on the
completion text (pre-tab)

## Changes

- **`split_completion_description()`** — new `pub(crate)` helper that
splits a raw completion string at `\t` into `(text, Vec<frames>)`
- **`Suggestion`** — added `description: Vec<String>` field;
`formatted()` (used for insertion) unchanged; added `with_description()`
builder
- **`post_process_completion()`** — strips description frames before
quoting/path-processing; attaches them via `with_description()`
- **`UnprocessedSuggestion::match_text()`** — for `Raw` items, returns
only text before the first `\t`
- **`SuggestionFormatted`** — added `description_spans` /
`description_frame_width`; `new()` takes a `frame_index: usize`;
`render()` appends description with a two-space separator;
`display_width` uses the widest frame for layout stability
- **`ActiveSuggestions::into_grid()`** — computes `frame_index` from
wall-clock time at 24 fps before building the grid

## Example

A completion function returning `--verbose\tEnable verbose output` will
display as:

```
--verbose  Enable verbose output
```

…where only `--verbose ` is inserted. With multiple frames
(`--verbose\t[=]\t[ ]`), the description cycles at 24 fps.

refactor: strip GH_TOKEN from CLI, post reviews from orchestrator (#45)

Closes #40

## Summary

Refactors the token architecture so the Copilot CLI **never** receives a
GitHub API token. This is a prerequisite for adding `contents:write` to
the GitHub App — without this change, the review CLI would inherit push
access (prompt injection risk).

### Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `SKILL.md` | CLI writes review JSON to `.copilot-review.json` instead
of calling `gh api` |
| `copilot.py` | Removed `gh_token` param; explicitly pops `GH_TOKEN`
from env |
| `review.py` | Reads review JSON from worktree, posts via
`post_review()`, inlines stats |
| `github.py` | Added `post_review()`; removed dead
`append_stats_to_review` and `count_bot_reviews` |

### Token flow (before → after)

**Before:** CLI gets App token as `GH_TOKEN` → calls `gh api` to post
review
**After:** CLI gets NO GitHub token → writes JSON file → orchestrator
posts via App token

### Next steps (after merge)

1. Add `contents:write` to the homelab-review-bot GitHub App
2. Re-trigger #38 (Prometheus metrics) with `/implement`

Map reqwest errors to semantically correct Twirp error codes (#314)

* Map reqwest errors to semantically correct Twirp error codes

Replace the blanket From<reqwest::Error> → InvalidArgument mapping with
variant-specific logic:

- is_builder() → InvalidArgument (actually bad input)
- is_redirect() / is_body() / is_decode() → Internal (unexpected behavior)
- everything else (connect, timeout, request) → Unavailable (transient)

application errors, enabling correct retry decisions.
